title: The Place the Prophet ﷺ Told Us Not to Enter
_note: CINEMATIC style: full-bleed Pexels footage (NO humans/faces/bodies — every clip verified by thumbnail), brand ivory/green/gold grade, calm captions, NO recital (Qur'an shown as Arabic+English, voice speaks English only). Simple English for non-native viewers. Sources verified: Sahih al-Bukhari 4702 & 3379-3381 (the 'enter only weeping' warning at al-Hijr on the way to Tabuk); Qur'an 7:73-79 & 15:80-84 (Thamud, Salih, the blast); modern luxury tourism — Royal Commission for AlUla, Banyan Tree AlUla, Maraya. TTS: al-Hijr='al-HIJR', Hegra='HEG-ra', Thamud='tha-MOOD', Salih='SAW-lih', Tabuk='ta-BOOK'. Pexels clips streamed at render (API key never committed). For #HistoryTikTokContest (1min+).

segments.text: In the deserts of Saudi Arabia, there is a place the Prophet Muhammad, peace be upon him, told us never to enter.
It is called al-Hijr. Today, tourists call it Hegra.
Long ago, a proud and powerful people called Thamud carved their homes straight into these mountains.
Allah sent them a prophet named Salih, to call them back to the truth. But they refused.
So the punishment came. One mighty blast, and by morning, the whole nation was gone.
Their empty homes still stand in the sand, silent for thousands of years.
Long after, the Prophet, peace be upon him, passed this place with his army, on the way to Tabuk.
He covered his face. He told his men to hurry, and not to rest here.
Then he gave a warning. Do not enter the homes of these people, he said, unless you are weeping.
If you cannot weep, do not enter at all. So that what struck them does not strike you.
But look at the very same place today. It has become a luxury holiday resort.

segments.foot: Banyan Tree AlUla · Maraya, Royal Commission for AlUla
segments.text: Five-star hotels. Concerts by famous singers. Influencers posing for photos.
But for a believer, this is not a place to relax. It is a sign. A warning from the past.
Travel through the land, and see how the people before you ended.
Some places are not made for holidays. They are made for lessons. May Allah guide us all.

sources: Sahih al-Bukhari 4702 (also 3379-3381; Sahih Muslim 2980) — at al-Hijr, on the way to Tabuk, the Prophet ﷺ said: 'Do not enter upon these people who were punished unless you are weeping; if you do not weep, do not enter, lest what struck them strike you.' He covered his head and hastened through the valley.
Sahih al-Bukhari 3380 — he urged his mount on and hurried past the dwellings of Thamud.
Qur'an, Surah Al-A'raf 7:73-79 — Thamud, the prophet Salih, and their destruction.
Qur'an, Surah Al-Hijr 15:80-84 — the people of al-Hijr; 15:82 'they carved homes from the mountains'; 15:83 'the shriek (blast) seized them at morning'.
Qur'an, Surah Ar-Rum 30:42 — 'Travel through the land and observe how was the end of those before' (shown as Arabic + English; NOT recited).
Modern luxury tourism: Royal Commission for AlUla; Banyan Tree AlUla (Ashar Valley); Maraya (world's largest mirrored building); celebrity concerts — verified via RCU/Accor and press, June 2026.
VISUALS: all Pexels clips were checked by thumbnail to contain NO human faces or bodies; the false 'ozone hole over AlUla' claim is excluded.
